entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: I do not get any other error.

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: I do not get any other error. We have SAP_ABA 750 and SAP_UI 754.

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: We have SAP_ABA 750 and SAP_UI 754..

entry.title: Re: sap.ui.table.table - Best way to clear table completely and rebind with different set of data ?
entry.content:
Hi,

After you rebind your second set do model refresh,

this.getView().byId("tableID").getModel("tableModel").refresh(true);
this.getView().byId("tableID").getModel("tableModel").updateBindings(true);

entry.title: Problema com envio do workitem para uma lista de usuários
entry.content:
Meu WF está com acoplamento de eventos e de instância realizados. WF está ativo. Tarefa de WF e de workitem ambas com a classificação geral para os usuários habilitados. Configuração da SWU3 OK.

Tenho uma regra de WF que encontra uma lista de usuários.  A tarefa de WF que usa essa regra é disparada, mas sempre desconsidera o primeiro usuário da lista anteriormente obtida.

Alguém sabe como resolver ?

entry.title: Re: Error ".txt is not imported prior to export" when pub...
entry.content: Thanks for the update that helps the community !

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: Initially I got the certificate error. Then, I used "ignoreCertError: true" in the UI5-deploy.yaml file and got rid of the certification error.

entry.title: Re: ABAP News for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2411
entry.content:
Hi @lenapadeken 

Thanks for the post. Those are great additions to ABAP.

 I think there's a mistake in this post regarding the READ TABLE + WHERE. The problem is that the code snippet is showing LOOP +  WHERE:

This post:

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content:
https://www.npmjs.com/package/nwabap-ui5uploader

your server is too old, probably not supported by fiori tool regarding deployment

use that node js module to do the deployment

entry.title: Re: Financial Accounting 4 Advanced Execution Service Scenario in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Kai,

First: this is just an option to provide a costing sheet on the service order item. you cyn control with derivation tool. The costing sheet derived from PM order as before,fits better to the processes running on the PM order. thus we do not inherit.

In the standard process there are revenues posted on the service order item. One could apply negative surcharges on the revenue amount to cover costs for e.g. expected commisions or insurance.
And you can post manual costs on the service order item.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Discrete Orders - Sampling Requirements (dif...
entry.content: @ManoelCosta - wondering if you have any expertise or ideas on this requirement implementing a routing in SAP DM.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors LMS: 1H 2023 Release Highlights
entry.content:
@nageshpolu @lauragarbi It is December 12th, 2024 - Hello Nagesh and Laura

Our company has recently expressed interest in implementing in 'SAP SuccessFactors Learning' #6 above which is: 'Receive Reminders of Learning Assignments in Microsoft Teams'.

Is there any 'SAP SuccessFactors Learning' KBA etc. or instructions on how to go about doing this integration of 'SAP SuccessFactors Learning' with Microsoft Teams, especially in regards specifically to 'Receive Reminders of Learning Assignments in Microsoft Teams'.

This is something that became available in 2023-1H-LMS release, and we are now sitting in the tail end of 2024, going into 2025 and my company would like to implement this, so I am hoping someone from SAP or the Community can point me in the right direction for the steps to do this.

I found an old Reference Number (WRK-233) on the What's New Viewer for the Release, but I am now not able to find anything regarding this topic.

Any help in providing me the instructions, or pointing me to where I can find them would be appreciated.

Thank you in advance - Mike Wright

entry.title: Re: Business Partner Authorization Tips and Tricks
entry.content:
With field groups, we can set most of the fields enabled / display only.

I'm still stuck with 1 requirement:

We want to make sure that only certain people can edit specific customer (sales) partner functions.
Not all of them!

is this possible? And if yes, how?

Best regards,
Hans

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content: @ClaudiaMayer when we can expect changes on BOX 27A of the T4 ?

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hi @ChandraV @Matt_Fraser @graziela @CarlosAccorsi 

We are in SAP HR 604 and recently upgraded support stacks from J8 to L0. During this upgrade we kept the TUB level at 147 Cyclic K2.

Now when we go to PUOC_10 transaction code in production, payment number are missing for some pay period that has retro. Please do let me know why this is happening and is there a corresponding note that resolves this issue.

i found a note "3295248" please do let me know if this is going to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: cost center missing in financial statements report (f...
entry.content:
Hi Krishna,

The reason it is not showing the 'Cost Center' in the financial statement report is because this report is expected to show only Balance sheet and Profit and Loss details.

It is the expected system architecture and in addition, it does not show both the 'Cost Center and Project Cost Structure' details in the  financial statement report.

Best Regards,

Abarna

entry.title: Configuring SAP Cloud Connector to send data to Splunk enterprise
entry.content: Is it possible to send data to splunk hec endpoint via cloud connector?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Custom Tile Translation is not working
entry.content:
Hi!

I have the same issue, running the program /UI2/PAGE_CACHE_SYNCHRONIZE solved my problem. This is not mentioned in the SAP Guideline.

Best regards!

entry.title: ADSSAP upgrade from 7.50 SP19 to SP31
Re: Selling Energy Using the Sales Order Integration into Utilities Sales Contracts
entry.content: Thx for Sharing

entry.title: Generating High Quality Leads with an SAP Business Network Catalog
entry.content:
SAP Business Network is a thriving business-to-business (B2B) ecosystem where over six trillion dollars are transacted annually. Millions of companies benefit from selling their products and services on the network by increasing their sales with existing customers, getting discovered by new customers and forming lasting business relationships. SAP Business Network catalog is a tool that companies can use to generate more leads and further grow their business on the network. 

What is a network catalog? 

A network catalog is a single master catalog that can be created via file upload. It is searchable by keyword and viewable by all buyers on SAP Business Network. Companies can include both products and services, regardless of how they are measured, priced, or delivered, in their catalog. Each catalog item includes fields for a name, description, photo, specs, price, and a link to the uploading supplier’s company profile. 

What are the benefits? 

  • Uploading a network catalog provides increased visibility for the company and their offerings, as well as high quality lead generation.  
  • By uploading a searchable network catalog, suppliers can share their entire catalog with all buyers on SAP Business Network.  
  • This allows suppliers to be able to upload and maintain all content in one source. 
  • These buyers are ready to buy the products and services offered by the supplier as they are searching for specific content on the network.  
  • Once a buyer finds a product or service they are interested in, they can send a request for information (RFI) via the price check button on the product listing, which will show up in the supplier’s ‘Leads’ tab within the portal. 
  • Suppliers can also get greater visibility for their company profile, which is linked directly from all network catalog items, where they can share more information illustrating why they are the best choice for the buyer such as certifications, sustainability ratings, and more. Learn more about the supplier profile here. 

Watch this video to learn more about the network catalog. 

Why upload a network catalog now? 

Currently, all companies with an SAP Business Network, enterprise account have full access to upload their products and services to their network catalog. Beginning in Q1, companies must purchase the SAP Business Network, promote subscription in order to unlock the full capabilities of the network catalog. So, if you’re an enterprise account supplier, take advantage of this opportunity while you can and enjoy cost savings! All existing network catalogs will be left as is, regardless of a promote subscription come Q1. 

Sign into your SAP Business Network, enterprise account to upload your network catalog today!

entry.title: Re: What's on Your SAP Community Wish List?
entry.content: @garshen  hello mate! well, i agree, for example i feel we have a better work life balance but J, waking up at 5ish in winter is a pain (yes i work in est)... whats your module? im a basis Guy

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello,

Regarding Payment number missing from OCWB, I noticed that in BT table of payroll results Date and time are not updated when it retroed, tables REGUH and REGUP are updated correctly.

Implemented note 3543393 but didn't help.

Thank you,

Dharitree Ravat

entry.title: Re: Custom Field not getting updated
entry.content: @ThaboMaroga, as per the Importing Custom Fields from Platform to Learning, the custom field configuration line formatting is different than the one that was shared. Additionally, check if there was any exception in the User Connector - SF run for the user who didn't have that custom field value added/updated.

entry.title: Brazil Localization for SAP GTS Product Classification
entry.content:
Hello Everyone, we are working on a project to implement SAP GTS in Brazil and are running into a issue with our product classification.  

For Brazil, Product classification consist of 3 parts.  The NCM code, Product Attributes and EX Codes (used mainly for Imports).  SAP GTS E4H allows the loading of the NCM library and product attributes from Siscomex but the EX code (Ex-Tarifário) we are seeing no way to maintain this data in SAP GTS.  And these EX codes are critical for certain NCMs to allow cheaper tax rates.  

Has anyone come up a with a solution to maintain these EX codes that would allow for the import declaration to be filed?  We are currently working with SAP but they are also unclear on a path forward as the Brazil localization is new and not really used yet.     

Thanks, Eric Perala

SAP Global Trade Services SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) for SAP S/4HANA SAP Tax Declaration Framework for Brazil

entry.title: Re: Publishing values to a recurring pay component from V...
entry.content: Laura, Does the Bonus Base need to be a recurring pay component. Because you are using it in the salary tab, there is no historical element involved, you are just taking a value as of the date you pull the data from EC. You could establish a non-recurring pay component and publish a value calculated in the summary line of the variable pay form and then pull the data from that pay component into the salary tab. The only downside is that you would need a PC like BonusBase2024 and then BonusBase2025, etc.

entry.title: Re: Data Dictionary for Learning
entry.content: @annasergi, please, access the SAP Help Portal > SAP SuccessFactors Learning landing page and login, otherwise you won't be able to find and download the data dictionary. After that, click the "Operate" tab > Scroll down the page to find the "Reference" entry > Click "Data Dictionary and Database Changes" to download a .zip file containing the information (KBA 2326249).

entry.title: Re: [Compensation] Unexpected Weighted Calculation
entry.content:
Hi @pwc_94 

Can you confirm whether you were able to fix your issue and if so what the solution was ?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements - Save Variant/Layout not working...
entry.content: You need add the reference <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/HTML5.xml"> <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.HTML5.v1" Alias="HTML5"/> </edmx:Reference>

Best Regards,

Vasu Sikhakolli

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: Hello @hemnth_kumar and @@dharitree_ravat These questions are related to off-cycle workbench so I recommend to you to check in a PY-XX forum or to open a case under this component. As the main topic of this forum is for Tax reporter.

entry.title: Learning History Import - Tips
entry.content:
As mentioned, the Learning History is a union of specifics entities in the system, where we can record the employee performance against the training.

There are three main entities as follow:

Item
User
Performance

Our job is grantee that you can record at employee history, all learning records acquired by him during your journey in the company.

The LMS receive the data through Connectors that are generally files in .txt or .csv format uploaded in the SFTP folder and imported by the system. There is several rules, required columns, formats, length and attributes that can be used to build the files and need to be observed prior the import process. I recommend to take a look in the official documentation to know more details >> 2318341 - LMS Connectors Knowledge Support and Tips - SAP for Me / Learning Item Connector | SAP Help Portal  / Purpose of User Connector - SF | SAP Help Portal  /  Learning History Connector | SAP Help Portal

To start my explanation, I'll separate in 3 Blocks as follow:

ITEM

This is one primary entity in the system where we can create structures to deliver knowledge. According our Workbook: "...Items are the building blocks of SuccessFactors Learning Management.  They are the assignable units that typically represent all forms of training activities, including online and instructor-led courses.  They may also be used to represent non-training activities such as meetings and certifications as well as material goods (such as workbooks) made available for Users for purchase..."

Items can be classified according your needs, basically we have Instructor-Led, Online, Instructor-Led with Online Content (formerly called Blended) and Other (i.e., physical goods) and External-Course.

When an Item is created the LMS will identify follow the schema:

The most important detail is check your primary system and create a relationship between him and the LMS, granting the the items can be delivery according the classification, delivery method and so on.

The import is delivered using the Item Data Connector.

USER

User is the person who will consumes the delivered items, according our workbook: "... Users are represented by database records which contain not only detailed demographic information (usually captured from an HR system of record) but also tied to a transcript of all completed training and non-training events (learning history)..."

The user identity could be different from your primary system and again, is very common you need to realize a correlation between then to acquired the right register.

The import is delivered using the SF User Data Connector. Look here in this IDP (Implementation Design Principles) >> https://d.dam.sap.com/a/ZgMShsd / User Data considerations when implementing Employe... - SAP Community

PERFORMANCE

Are the intersection between Item and User, that means the performance obtained by the User during the knowledge session delivered within Item. The results will appear in the Learning History user session, where the certificate can be downloaded and content reviewed.

The import is delivered by Learning History Connector.

Now that we know the main entities to build a Learning History, let's discuss some points before start the import properly.

Importing Active / Inactive Items
If you planning to use the Items imported in your system to future sessions, is very common to import the item with the maximum information required.
Pay attention in do not associate the surveys before the import process, because if there is a survey associated, for each event recorded the user will receive the survey to fill, so run the Learning History  Connector first and when finalize, add the surveys (if required) in the item connector and update your items running the connector again.
Be careful with Custom Columns values, because if your columns has a reference fields, for each value imported the LMS will assume that is a new item for your list.
If you have online content to import together, there is a couple of connectors that you can use to do, so look into documentation >> 2568384 - How to mass upload content files to iContent and create entities? - SAP for Me. 
Remember to use in this connector for each column the separator "pipe" ( | ) , "double quotes" ("") to identify text inside the columns, REV_DTE in the format (MON-DD-YYYY HH24:MI:SS ex: JAN-01-2024 23:59:59) and the end of line as !##!
In case of import inactive, only the required fields need to be imported. Look with special attention if you're trying to import items with several revisions, in this case check the documentation.
Most common errors and how to fix it:
3529663 - Connectors - Unescaped quote detected Error - SAP for Me
2642533 - LMS: Cannot specify a NULL Key value for reference entity REV_DTE in Item Connector - SAP for Me
2166405 - "Inactive revisions are not supported" Exception - Item Connector - SAP for Me
2555746 - Item Connector - The new revision date must be after the previous revision - SAP for Me

Importing Active / Inactive Users
Independently if you are importing Active or Inactive Users, if is in the system the item and the event, so the LMS will add one line to the employee Learning History.
Active users importing is most recurrent requisition, once time that the LMS will be used as primary Learning Resource and it'll use to training. Be avoid with custom columns to bring data to build the Assignment Profile rules, locale, time zone and Domain
Importing Inactive Users is only for history purposes, it's means that it depends directly from the legal requirements that you company is inserted, because some kind of report could be required to check the Learning History or employee performance. Some few fields are required to import the inactive users, be aware with the field that controls the employee status and the termination date if is required.
Most common errors and how to fix it:
2668004 - BizX to LMS User Connector - SF Troubleshooting - SAP for Me
2316465 - TERM_DTE < HIRE_DTE not triggering SF User Connector Exception Message - SAP for Me
2543989 - User Connector SF failing with : Error Entity Invalid. No transformation data configured. NOTACTIVE: active - SAP for Me
2273500 - LMS User Connector or SF User Connector - Gender Exception error - SAP for Me
2396678 - Error code 1102 - "Entity does not exist.LocaleID:" - User Connector - SF - SAP for Me

Learning History Import
Be aware with the date format to record the Event (MON-DD-YYYY HH24:MI:SS), Completion Status and time zone.
Not more to add here except the fact that once time the record is imported, you can't delete massively, in case to observe some inconsistency.
The only way to delete a duplicated or incorrect record, is manually one-by-one or requesting to SAP to run a script to delete it (Costs Involved) >> 2436676 - Mass Updating/Deleting Learning History Records - SAP for Me
Most common errors and how to fix it:
2297869 - Learning history grade - SAP for Me
3095018 - Learning History Connector Exception - SAP for Me
3540533 - Learning History doesn't show results depending on the user's locale - SAP for Me
2699610 - Import learning history - Not a valid date or time in the format 'MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss a' - SAP for Me

IMPORTING

Generally the order to import is:

User, Item and finally the Learning History.

To schedule the jobs the path is System Administration >> Connectors >> User Connector - SF, Item Connector and Learning History Connector.

OTHER TOPICS

Depending of the number of lines to import, you can slice the archives and import it, please look in the documentation >> 2256304 - Enabling multiple input files for Connectors - SAP for Me

Remember, keep the files to be imported in UTF-8 encoding.

You can download the connector`s template directly in the system using the path >> System Administration >> Connectors >> Download Connector Template.

Always use the TEST INSTANCE for your first imports with few lines to gain  expertise and confidence.

Here you can find interesting articles regarding Architecture Leading Practices >> Architecture Leading Practices | SAP SuccessFactors | SAP Community please look in the Data Migration Strategy.

CONCLUSION

Import entities to create a Learning History, is not a trivial task, requires planning, dedication and attention. Some problems can be avoided during this process, so take a time to read the TIPS shared and start your journey.

Remember to check with frequency the principal channel >> SAP SuccessFactors | SAP Community where you can find webinars, blogs, product releases, FAQs and so on.

Thanks for reading.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Thank you @graziela.

This has started happening after support stacks were applied so we thought of reaching out.

entry.title: Re: LMS-IAS integration without any Learning-only users
entry.content: @Yeonsu-Cho, as it stands, the "SAP Cloud Identity Services Identity Authentication is activated" requirement would be related to Learning only users. The "Learning Home Page Readiness Information" area has the following text: "For Learning only users with an Internal Shopping Account Type, SAP Cloud Identity Services Identity Authentication must be activated". "If IAS is not integrated, are there any side effects?" - If there are no Learning only users in the environment, the fact that the Learning-IAS implementation isn't in place wouldn't impact any user (they'd be all authenticating via BizX-IAS, as I understand).

entry.title: CAP Facade entity for SAP HANA Cloud view (or virtual view) with association
entry.content:
We have a need to consume an SAP HANA Cloud view from CAP.

We can access the view with the regular config (hdb synonyms & grants + facade entity)

but what if the view has association, is it supported by the CAP facade entity?

My other question would be if this view comes from an old backend via a virtual table in SAP HANA Cloud, would we be able to expose the CDS view association and proxy them over the facade entity in CAP?

thanks

entry.title: Re: Document parameters are not correct from sap bo rest api
entry.content: Hi Joe, can you send me sample rest api request for this? How can I get parameters with your advice using rest api?

entry.title: Re: Employee clocking in at 11:30PM for a midnight shift
entry.content: Thank you Volker! Appreciate the clarity. My only feedback is that it would be great if SuccessFactors could make these types of limitations (based on the backlog) available somehow so that customers/consultants can be made aware. That said, in my experience the SF Time is one of the best teams at SF at communicating system behavior to the customer/consultant community. Keep up the good work!

entry.title: Re: Vendor Upload File in Ariba Network
entry.content: Can you try accessing this link: https://support.ariba.com/doc/KB/123428_01__VUF_Introduction.mp4

entry.title: Re: Vendor Upload File in Ariba Network

entry.title: Re: Attachments on Remittance or Payment status information
entry.content: Standard process for sending remittance information is SAP ERP-->SAP BTP (or CIG)-->SAP Business Network-->Ariba Buying & Invoicing Management

entry.title: Re: Group Assignments Based on User Attributes – a Flexible Solution for Managing Conditional & Risk
entry.content: I am facing similar issue while trying to use multiple condition statement for different group assignment. It seems that only the last condition works and overwrite all earlier conditions. Any help is much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Invoices in Business Network failing incorrectly
entry.content:
Hello @Velu_V , 

Based on my understanding, i have seen this error generally in two scenarios:
1) The invoice was rejected because there is another invoice already identified by the same payloadID. Each document in SAP Business Network must have their unique payloadID, they cannot share the same payloadID
2) The invoice got stuck in Ariba Buying & Invoicing with no entity reference number

I guess this would require support team to review logs and trace so i think SR with support is the right option to get this fixed.

entry.title: sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 licence problem
entry.content:
I am trying to create a container in Docker Desktop, and after entering the command:

docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -i --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 -agree-to-sap-license -skip-limits-check

I encounter an issue with the license, even though the "Days to expire" is 491. Can anyone advise me on how to proceed?

C:\Users\Mizi4> docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -i --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 -v C:/Users/Mizi4/license.key:/opt/sap/ASABAP_license sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 -agree-to-sap-license -skip-limits-check

You have accepted the SAP Developer Center Software Developer License Agreement
by passing the parameter -agree-to-sap-license.
You can read the license text in the file /SAP_COMMUNITY_DEVELOPER_License
in the image.
Hint: docker exec -it a4h less /SAP_COMMUNITY_DEVELOPER_License


WARNING: the following system limits are below recommended values:
(sysctl kernel.shmmni = 4096) < 32768
(sysctl vm.max_map_count = 262144) < 2147483647
(sysctl fs.file-max = 1517899) < 20000000
(sysctl fs.aio-max-nr = 65536) < 18446744073709551615
Hint: consider adding these parameters to your docker run command:
--sysctl kernel.shmmni=32768
Hint: if you are on Linux, consider running the following system commands:
sudo sysctl vm.max_map_count=2147483647
sudo sysctl fs.file-max=20000000
sudo sysctl fs.aio-max-nr=18446744073709551615

start hostcontrol using profile /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/host_profile

entry.title: For Central Finance, can my source ERP have a different fiscal year than S4?
entry.content:
Hi All,

This questions is for those of you who have implement Central Finance as a stepping stone to S4.

I have a scenario where our source SAP ECC system which will feed into Central Finance has a fiscal year that need to be fixed to line up to the Corporate fiscal year.  In general, ECC is 1 day off each month end from the Corporate calendar.

Is it possible to load this ECC data into Central Finance and have S4 Finance on the corrected Corporate fiscal year?  

While correcting the ECC calendar might seem like the easier approach, there are a ton of dependencies to consider including open amortizing Rev Rec scheduled(maybe a million or more sales documents) that would require updating, thousands upon thousands of scheduled jobs and dependent variants that would need updating, and lastly the big reason why the ECC calendar was changed originally, the month end settlement jobs take upwards of 18hours to complete, moving them a day would mean a 1 day delay in the overall month end close.

Consultants are telling me we should just keep the existing ECC fiscal year and use that in S4 for these Company Codes.  That does not seem right, this is our change in S4 to correct this process and carrying over a bad business process just seems wrong for a solution that is designed specifically to integrate/replicate data from multiple ERP's in a single S4 instance, new and improved, not carry over all the bad stuff from legacy systems.

Ideas and details are appreciated

 

thanks

Nathan

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - eDocument WS Provider ?
entry.content:
Thanks for your feedback.

Indeed, I found some interesting information, such as :
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/95a7320cd4054d4e9fed6b5b70f3d004/ff3bdc136ade467c87f4ad789c6bd78e.html?latest

But on the whole, I find that the documentation on this subject is very light.

Using middleware is an option, Integration Suite is in our landscape.

In my situation, the final purpose is to create the Invoice in VIM (Opentext).
In the end, I'm not sure about using SAP DRC in this context.

entry.title: Re: Need to delete serial number from outbound delivery created in MIGO
entry.content:
"DELETE_SERNR_LS" calls "SERNR_DEL_FROM_DOCUMENT"

but in order to actually delete you need to follow up with "SERIAL_LISTE_POST_LS"

and then update action such as commit work

entry.title: Re: PR/PO Creation with Mix of Service and Material Items
entry.content:
Hello @Kuntanawong 

Did you tried creating service request or ticket with SAP Support for CIG custom mapping with this issue?

entry.title: Milestones on Other Details tab missing on PM form using Latest Goal Management
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there anything different when using the latest goal management and milestones?  I am able to create a goal  plan with milestones and they appear when viewing the goal plan but the Other Details tab on the PM form is empty.

Milestone in goal plan XML:

<field-definition id="milestones" type="table" required="false" detail="false" viewdefault="off" showlabel="true" field-show-coaching-advisor="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<field-label>Milestones</field-label>
<field-description>Milestones</field-description>
<table-row-label lang="en_US">Milestones</table-row-label>
<table-column id="start" type="date" required="false" validate-start-before-due="true" cascade-update="push-down">
<column-label>Start Date</column-label>
</table-column>
<table-column id="desc" type="textarea" required="false" cascade-update="push-down">
<column-label>On Track or Off Track?</column-label>
</table-column>
</field-definition>

 

Plan layout:

 

I do have the the <form-layout> in the goal plan xml.

 

Below is a sample code:

<td valign="top" width="20">#if ($display.metric-lookup-table) <b>${label.metric-lookup-table}:</b> ${field.metric-lookup-table} #end</td>

And in the form temples, the other details tab is enabled for all roles for all steps.

But the Other Details tab has no content on the pm form.

What is missing to prevent the milestones data to appear on the Other Details tab on the PM form?

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Managed Gateway for Spend&Network unable to connect t...
entry.content:
Hello @anilpb00957 ,

The error indicates that the targeted interface is not ready to handle the request either because it's inactive or was not generated correctly, or due to due to system refresh/transport in ERP client end.It is also possible that the error message is usually raised when the ICF(Internet Communication Frameworks) External aliases are not created in the ERP.

You can use the following ERP transaction code to identify the ICF External Alias (/sap/bc/srt/xip/arba/documentstatusupdaterequest/<client>) is available in Tcode SICF from your SAP ERP system.

If the above Alias exists, Run Tcode SRT_LOG to check any Error logs happened within Managed Gateway for Spend&Network connection test.
In this step if you see "No web service configuration for this access path: "/ap/bc/srt/xip/arba/documentstatusupdaterequest/xxx?MessageId=xxxxxxx" was issued, then that means it is necessary to generate the inbound Document Status Update interface through the SAP Business Network Interface Setup for IDoc/Web Service option
(Menu path: Integration with Other SAP Components > SAP Business Network Integration > General Settings > Set Up the Interface > Configure the Connections to Send Messages) from Tcode SPRO in your SAP ERP system.

Regards,
Mridul Shukla

entry.title: Any suggestions and solutions please? It's an urgent requirement. Thank you.
Re: Withholding tax percentage to change if the invoice a...
entry.content: Any further suggestions please?

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Cloud Connector to send data to Splun...
entry.content:
Hello @RishiSuthar 

I think you cannot directly use the SAP Cloud Connector to "send data" to Splunk, you can leverage it to facilitate the process by creating a secure connection between your on-premise SAP systems and a cloud-based integration platform like SAP Cloud Integration, which then has the capability to send data to Splunk

Regards,
Mridul

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Cockpit Error : instance already exist...
entry.content:
Dear experts,

As an addition, the KBA below explains exactly this concept, that the Migration Cockpit should be used for initial data load, not mass data upload:

  • 2650201 - Migration Cockpit: How to create/update data records

Also, since the documentation was mentioned in the answer above but wasn't attached, I'm linking it here for reference:

  • Product - extend existing record by new org levels | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Caio

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Cloud Connector to send data to Splun...
entry.content:
Hello, you can refer below document which means SAP Integration Suite provides Splunk Adapter:

Splunk Adapter | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Comparison Tool: Reducing Errors and Saving Time
entry.content:
Have you ever wished for a tool to compare the Company and Site Configurations between your SAP Fieldglass tenants, like when you’re moving configs from test to production? What about simplifying your work and minimizing problems when moving configurations?

Well, if you’re a Configuration Manager user, your dreams have come true!

We introduce you to the Comparison Tool!

          

This tool is designed to help you streamline your operations by letting you compare company and site configurations between tenants effortlessly.  

Why use the Comparison Tool? 

Check out some benefits of this feature:

  • Highlights only the differences between the two tenants, rather than displaying all configurations.
  • Reduces the chance of mistakes during production transition, simplifying the process.
  • Decreases the need for full testing before go-live, saving you time and resources.
  • Helps to identify and solve issues before go-live, avoiding post-deployment problems.

You can access the Comparison Tool tile from the Configuration Manager Self-Service Dashboard.

ℹ️For information about how to use the Comparison Tool, see Adding Environment Details.

Added Bonus!

You can download the comparison results in a .csv format, this will help you to visualize the data and facilitate your analysis.

Start using the Comparison Tool and see by yourself how it facilitates your work, saves you time and effort!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Split Accounting Behavior in Ariba Buying...
entry.content:
Hello @omar_hassan99 ,

I think this behavior is correct since when split accounting is done in SAP Ariba B&I which means single account is split in different IO or WBS so it should send "2 Distribute" to SAP ERP and if no split is done then "1 Distribute" should be updated in Accounting tab.
Hence, this is working as expected

entry.title: Re: Document parameters are not correct from sap bo rest api
entry.content: Hi Joe, thank you soo much. Lastly, How do you know about this usage? If you use any documentation or another source, can you send a link?

entry.title: Re: BODS BOE scheduler failed to run with error: "Running...
entry.content:
@mengzhnag 

Please make sure that OS Credentials are maintained in CMC > Applications > Central Management Server > Program Object Rights 

Alternatively you can maintain the OS Credentials in SAP Data Services Management Console > Management > CMS Connection > 

Then recreate the schedule.

Regards Joe

LO_HTTP_CLIENT->REQUEST->SET_CDATA( LV_STRING ).

entry.title: What is the bank key best for Uganda, Botswana, Dominican Republic and Guatemala?
entry.content:
Hi all.

I have to create the bank master data (tc FI01) for Uganda, Botswana, Dominican Republic and Guatemala. What is the best practice for the bank key for each of these countries?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward, as of November 2023
entry.content:
I don't see any other ETL replacing Data Services as far as SAP Data Migration is concerned. I know about other tool set like ADM/ADMM  and those are good tools but those are not real ETL tools. The capability of those tools are limited as far as transformation, data quality and loading is concerned. BODS is mature tool in non SAP to SAP, non SAP/SAP to cloud based heavy data loads and data integrations with its native integration. Other big factor is the performance, no other ETL comes close to the performance capability of this tool with multiple options to optimize.

In my opinion, SAP should move this tool to BTP and sell it as a Service, there are thousands of people using this tool for so long (used to be called Acta), there is a huge community for it, it will be really foolish of SAP to sunset this.

entry.title: Re: How does SAP SLT send messages to Web Services?
entry.content:
@mengzhnag 

The supported method of replicating SAP SLT using SAP Data Services is ODP which offers you near real-time replication. By near real-time what i mean is that the Job in SAP Data Services will still run as a batch job, which you can schedule to run at a certain frequency e.g. every 15 minutes.

First perform the initial load and then after the initial load as completed, you should enable the Change Data Capture (CDC) and schedule the batch job to run after every X minutes(s)/hour(s). 

If there are any recent changes in SLT they will then be processed and loaded to your target PostgreSQL target.

Please see the link below for more information

  • Replicating Data to SAP Data Services
  • Read, browse, and import SAP ODP sources

 

Regards,

Joe

entry.title: Can old BW 731 system consume data from EC Payroll into SAP BW by utilizing these OData Services?
entry.content:
Hi, Experts:

We need to transfer data from new EC Payroll system into our old BW system with release 731 with Support Pack level 30 (non-HANA). Since SAP has stated that RSA extractor is no longer supported, we won't be able to use Datasources (tcode RSA6).  OData services is one of the options we are considering. Does anyone know whether an old BW 731 system can consume data from EC Payroll into SAP BW by utilizing OData Services? What are the minimum system requirements for BW to consume data as the target system use OData Services? Any information of how to do it will be appreciated! Or any other options besides OData Services for complex data requirements in source system?

Thanks!

Jenny

entry.title: S4H to CPI. Need help
entry.content:
Hi friends. 

I'm following this blog (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-s4h-idoc-to-sap-cpi-inbound-idoc-interface/ba-p/13484455) and I'm getting stuck at step 6 of this blog. I'll include a picture to see if someone can help me figure out what am I doing wrong. 

When I try to add message type in the partner profile, the receiver port is not showing the CPI port. See screenshot attached. I have created port similar to how this blog shows in step 5. I'm assuming the port created in step 5 is what should be displayed in the receiver port in WE20, correct?

entry.title: Re: Upgrade failed to FP2408 SAP B1 for HANA
entry.content: check the log files at /var/log/SAPBusinessOne/

entry.title: Re: Does datasphere work good with ECC or should it be wi...
entry.content:
If you need to use ECC 6.0 as the source for Datasphere, it is strongly recommended to use BW Bridge. The reasons are:

1.Lack of CDS View Support: ECC 6.0 does not support CDS views, meaning data extraction must rely on BCT (Business Content Extractors).

2.Delta Extraction Issues: Many key sources in ECC do not have primary keys (PK), which can lead to challenges in maintaining delta consistency during data extraction. Using BW Bridge helps address these limitations by providing a more structured framework for handling data from ECC.

In the case of S/4HANA, delta can be handled perfectly using CDS views, so there is no need to use BW Bridge.

All the best,

Youngseol

entry.title: Re: Compatibility of SAP ECC version 6 EHP 5 with SAP Dat...
entry.content:
It is possible, but if you need to use ECC 6.0 as the source, it is recommended to use BW Bridge. Since CDS views are not supported, data extraction relies solely on BCT sources, and most major sources do not have primary keys (PK), which can lead to issues with delta extraction.

 

In the case of S/4HANA, delta can be handled perfectly using CDS views, so there is no need to use BW Bridge.

All the best,

entry.title: Re: Compatibility of SAP ECC version 6 EHP 5 with SAP Dat...
entry.content:
It is possible, but if you need to use ECC 6.0 as the source, it is recommended to use BW Bridge. Since CDS views are not supported, data extraction relies solely on BCT sources, and most major sources do not have primary keys (PK), which can lead to issues with delta extraction.

In the case of S/4HANA, delta can be handled perfectly using CDS views, so there is no need to use BW Bridge.

All the best,

Youngseol

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:

HR_BEN_US_ADJUST_CATCHUP_LIMIT

If that hurdle can be gotten past, we also noticed that the delivered payroll function LIMIT doesn't observe the same catchup limit adjustment details, so we get the 11,250 in the new function with an appropriate bplan, but then it gets undone back to the 7,500 with the LIMIT payroll function.

Has anyone else dealt with this issue or fixed this same issue?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Updated Transport to Production Strategy for Rapid Activation
entry.content:
If you are operating with SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA, you will need to transport your launchpad content, layout, and other configuration settings from your Development to your Production environment. You can now find an updated document with the latest advice on the transport strategy for SAP Fiori Rapid Activation and related SAP Fiori objects at

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Rapid Activation Transport Move to Production Strategy

 

What is SAP Fiori Rapid Activation?

As a reminder, the SAP Fiori Rapid Activation procedure lets you, as a system administrator, quickly activate the capabilities of the SAP Fiori launchpad and SAP Fiori applications using a series of task lists.

Refer to

  • As explained in the central note for all SAP S/4HANA releases, SAP Note 2902673 - Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA - Overview
  • SAP Help - SAP Fiori Overview - Rapid Activation of SAP Fiori
  • SAP Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Activate SAP Fiori in 1 day or less - the video playlist and FAQ
  • SAP Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Creating your custom business roles – the end-to-end process and video playlist

What’s changed in this version?

This document has the latest information for SAP S/4HANA 2023 release including

  • Considerations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers
  • Clearer explanation of how transport of Rapid Activation works
  • Prerequisites for transporting Rapid Activation
  • Which task lists to run on implementation versus upgrade
  • Mandatory/Optional tasks to run in Development versus subsequent systems
  • Critical parameters for transports
  • Coverage of new task list SAP_FIORI_FCM_CATALOG_ACTIVATION
  • How to transport settings in other Rapid Activation tools such as Launchpad configuration parameters in transaction /UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF

For official guidance for your SAP S/4HANA release and FPS, always refer to the Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA <release> <FPSxx>" for your SAP S/4HANA release and FPS (example: SAP Note 3469488 - Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02)

The links to these SAP notes are available in the document.

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru


You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA


Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG

entry.title: S4 Hana converter tx z to app fiori
entry.content:
Dears:

 

Please S4 Hana on Promise converter tx z to app fiori??

Manual Step a Step

Thank you

entry.title: Identity Provisioning not creating users after a read/sync no errors
entry.content:
So I'm trying to replicate SAP ECC Business Suite users to SAP Workzone Standard on BTP and have walked through a number of blogs and SAP Help documents to get everything setup.  When I run the job on the SAP Cloud Identity Services provisioning ECC source I see the users coming over and getting processed.  The results are mostly updated/created/read with a few errors which are expected, given the source is a development system with missing emails and other data for some users.

Now, the issue is that NO USERS/GROUPS are created in my custom identity provider as expected and there are no errors or logs that would help explain why they weren’t.  Without any specific errors I have no thread to pull on to try and get down to the root cause.

I’ve included some configuration screenshots for reference, which was gathered from the SAP Help documentation.  Transformations were left untouched which I believe is an important point.

Finally, for simplicity I have added a filter to bring in a single user from the source so that tests can be more quickly iterated; the results are below.

 


Source Configuration:

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Business Content MM
entry.content: Unfortunately, there is no usable MM content available yet. Currently, only SD, FI, CO, PM, and AM content exists.

entry.title: Re: Redraw a MDK Dynamic Page
entry.content:
@bill_froelich

I think there are some issue when the Dynamic Page is inside a Tab of Tabs Page.

First of all, when I navigate from a normal page to a Tabs Page, the rule for the Dynamic Page creation is triggered.

Unfortunately if the Dynamic Page is inside a Tab that is not immediately showed, if the User does not click that Tab where the dynamic page is contained and I need to set some text for some controls of that page with a Rule during an Action, this is not possible because the controls of the page does not exist yet.

The evaluateTargetPath fails:

clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderTexts/#Control:SectionedTable0").getControls()

If the User click on the Tab where the dynamic page is contained, the Rule for the Dynamic Page creation is triggered again and the above evaluateTargetPath works fine.

The second issue is when I try to set controls before the Dynamic Page Tab click with this code: 

clientAPI.getPageProxy().getAppClientData().HeaderTexts.forEach((text) => {
    let targetPath = "";
    targetPath = "#Page:CreateSalesOrderHeaderTexts/#Control:FCNote_" + text.ID;
    if (clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI(targetPath) == undefined) {
        return;
    }
    clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI(targetPath).setValue(text.Value);
    clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI(targetPath).setEditable(text.Editable);
});

In this case the Rule for Dynamic Page creation, after Tab click, does no longer triggered and the page results completely blank.

The current workaround is to add this code on OnPress Event of the Tab. With this workaround the rule for the Dynamic Page creation is triggered correctly and the controls can be set correctly.

Why this happens ?

entry.title: Re: Rise(private edition) have HANA HA(system replication...
entry.content:
Hi John,

The short answer is yes, it does.

I have supported over 30 customers in ECS, now referred to as "RISE with SAP," and implementing High Availability (HA) for every HANA database has always been essential.

Since customers can choose their preferred hyperscaler, like Azure, there is an inherent feature called auto recovery after failure, which serves as the first layer of protection:

"The Azure platform is designed to handle host node issues with minimal impact on VM performance. When a host node encounters a problem, Azure first attempts the least disruptive recovery method, which is to reboot the host. If rebooting the host isn't possible or if the original issue is hardware-related, the platform service heals all VMs on the affected host to a healthy node."

Once this step is passed with no positive result, the HA configuration kicks off to ensure system continuity. If the customer has also purchased a Disaster Recovery (DR) solution, it provides a further layer of protection as the next step in the process.

To my knowledge, HA is the standard choice for production environments only in RISE, while DR is an optional component that can be included in the Bill of Materials (BOM). For non-production environments, additional agreement may be necessary, depending on cost considerations to have the DR.

Please reach out to your Customer Delivery Manager (CDM) or Technical Service Manager (TSM) in RISE for more detailed information.

Thanks,

@Amin_Omidy

entry.title: Re: Credit Limit Request Configuration - SAP S4Hana Credit Management
entry.content:
@GOWRESHANHARAN 

Thank you for the detailed article, i followed it completely and configured our system, I am using new Approval Hierarchy process for CLR approval, which works fine if I initiate the CLR request from the app (Manage credit limit request ) or from UKM_CASE, both works and when i approve the case, it updates the CL in master data as well.

but when i directly update CL in BP, it moves the value to CL requested field (as mentioned by SAP), but i expect it to create CLR request, but it does not, am I missing something? for below customer i entered 420K CL which moved to requested field, now when i look for case in UKM_CASE or in the CLR app, i dont find it, i would expect it to be there.

 

Thanks for all the help the community.
